findings.evidence: `FourierConvention.lean:112-126` has `[BorelSpace E]` then an extra `[OpensMeasurableSpace E]`; `CharFunPositiveDefinite.lean:87-96` states the analogous paired charFun theorem without it; `BorelSpace/Basic.lean:184-187` defines `instance BorelSpace.opensMeasurable ... : OpensMeasurableSpace α`.
findings.fix: Drop the explicit `[OpensMeasurableSpace E]` binder from this theorem; the proof should still find it from `[BorelSpace E]`, matching `continuous_charFun_and_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg`.
findings.issue: `continuous_fourierIntegral_one_and_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg` adds `[OpensMeasurableSpace E]` even though the same theorem already assumes `[BorelSpace E]`, and Mathlib provides `BorelSpace.opensMeasurable`.

summary: One wrapper theorem carries a redundant measurable-space assumption instead of matching the nearby characteristic-function API’s natural Borel-level signature.
